# Beudox HR Frontend Application - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

This is a modern React-based frontend application for Human Resources Management, built with TypeScript and following component-driven architecture patterns. The application integrates with Supabase for backend services including database, authentication, and real-time features.

## Technology Stack & Patterns

### Core Technologies
- **React 18** with functional components and hooks
- **TypeScript** with strict configuration
- **Vite** for fast development and optimized builds
- **Tailwind CSS** with custom design tokens
- **React Router** for client-side routing
- **React Query** for server state management
- **Supabase** for backend-as-a-service

### Key Architectural Decisions
- **Component Composition**: Modular, reusable components with clear separation of concerns
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic extracted into reusable hooks (useAuth, useToast, etc.)
- **Type Safety**: Comprehensive TypeScript usage with generated Supabase types
- **Performance Optimization**: React Query caching, lazy loading, and optimized re-renders
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with Tailwind CSS

## Key Files & Components

### Application Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Main application bootstrap with React 18, router setup, and query client
- `src/App.tsx`: Root component with authentication routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page

### Layout System
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper with sidebar and content area
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Header with user menu and notifications
- `src/components/layout/UserMenu.tsx`: User dropdown menu
- `src/components/layout/NotificationBell.tsx`: Real-time notification indicator

### Core Business Components
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceUploadFlow.tsx`: Complex attendance import workflow with AI parsing
- `src/components/employee-profile/AttendanceTab.tsx`: Employee attendance history display
- `src/components/leave/ApplyLeaveModal.tsx`: Leave request form
- `src/components/payroll/PayrollSummary.tsx`: Payroll data visualization
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee search and selection

### Utility & Configuration
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Attendance time formatting functions
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control utilities
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Auto-generated database types

## Data Flow & State Management

### Server State
- **React Query** handles all API calls with caching, background refetching, and optimistic updates
- Query keys follow pattern: `['entity', id, filters]`
- Mutations use optimistic updates where appropriate

### Client State
- **React hooks** for local component state
- **useToast** for global toast notifications
- **useAuth** for authentication state

### Authentication
- Supabase Auth integration
- Role-based access control with granular permissions
- Automatic token refresh and session management

## Database Schema (Key Tables)

### Core Entities
- `employees`: Employee personal and job information
- `attendance_records`: Daily attendance data with timestamps
- `leave_requests`: Leave applications and approvals
- `payroll_records`: Salary and overtime calculations
- `projects`: Project management with team assignments

### Configuration Tables
- `company_settings`: Organization-wide settings
- `departments`: Department hierarchy
- `roles`: User roles and permissions
- `leave_types`: Configurable leave categories
- `evaluation_parameters`: Performance review criteria

## Component Patterns

### UI Components (src/components/ui/)
- Built on Radix UI primitives
- Consistent theming with CSS custom properties
- Accessible by default
- Composable and customizable

### Business Components
- Feature-specific components in dedicated directories
- Props interfaces for type safety
- Error boundaries and loading states
- Responsive design considerations

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for complex forms
- Zod schemas for validation
- Controlled components with proper error display

## Performance Considerations

### Optimization Techniques
- **Code Splitting**: Route-based and component lazy loading
- **Image Optimization**: Next.js Image component equivalent patterns
- **Bundle Analysis**: Vite build analysis for optimization
- **Caching Strategy**: React Query with appropriate stale times
- **Virtual Scrolling**: For large lists (implemented in some components)

### Bundle Size
- Tree shaking enabled
- Dynamic imports for heavy components
- Minimal dependencies with focused functionality

## Development Workflow

### Build & Development
- **Vite** for fast HMR and optimized production builds
- **TypeScript** strict checking
- **ESLint** with React and accessibility rules
- **Vitest** for unit testing
- **Playwright** for E2E testing

### Code Quality
- Pre-commit hooks for linting and formatting
- TypeScript strict mode
- Component documentation via TypeScript interfaces
- Consistent naming conventions

## Integration Points

### Supabase Integration
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- Database functions for complex operations
- Storage for file uploads

### External APIs
- AI service for attendance data parsing
- Email service for notifications
- File processing services

## Security Considerations

### Frontend Security
- Input validation and sanitization
- XSS prevention through React's built-in escaping
- CSRF protection via Supabase
- Secure token handling

### Data Protection
- Role-based access control
- Data encryption at rest (Supabase)
- Audit logging for sensitive operations

## Deployment & Infrastructure

### Build Process
- Vite production build with asset optimization
- Environment-specific configurations
- CDN deployment for static assets

### Environment Variables
- Supabase configuration
- Feature flags
- External service endpoints

This application represents a comprehensive HR management solution with modern React patterns, strong type safety, and scalable architecture.
